mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences despite concurrent growth in both series.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during periods of rising industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.3% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation months over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0% validates macroeconomic stability and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following strong GDP prints are negative in >50% of cases over a 15-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening business investment, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of consecutive decline periods despite confirmed output contraction.
